evidence.text: Good pay and benefits, good place to retire, nothing new. Good pay and benefits but no upward trajectory

- a lot of benefits, discounts etc - Apple Stock - the people you work with is what makes you want to stay - perfect for students looking for part time work but all ages and backgrounds are welcomed and encouraged. Good Pay and Benefits for Retail

evidence.role: Former employee - AppleCare Senior Technical Advisor
evidence.text: Competitive pay Lots of onsite conveniences like gym, cafe, coffee shop, medical Best employee training of any company Iâve worked for Lots of collaboration/teamwork for a call center Upper management was great, they communicated with more than just their direct reports Great benefits and management, but no work/life balance

Personal benefit's, stock plan, health care, child care Great benefits / Poor career growth

Apple is still an incredible company with world-class talent, strong benefits, and the opportunity to work on products and systems that operate at massive global scale. The people on the groundâengineers, operators, program managersâare deeply committed, smart, and mission-driven. When teams are empowered, the impact can be incredibly rewarding. Great Benefits, Good place to work depending on dept
RSU and a decent tech salary Limited Career Growth
Exposure to large scale projects Good for career growth
Lots of responsibility and career growth Great company to work
Fun work environment with diverse demographics. Good benefits even for part-time specialists. Great growth opportunities through Career Experience program Great work environment

Collaborative work culture, helpful teammates, exposure to real-world tools and workflows, flexible work environment, and opportunity to take ownership of tasks. Supportive team with opportunities to learn
good colleagues, they are very helpful Good colleagues, many restrictions
Discounts, external offers from partner companies, location and pay Good benefits, strange work ethics

Work-life balance is nonexistent, pay is poor compared to other tech companies, pressure from mgmt and subsequent turnover is extremely high. Expectations are ridiculous compared to compensation. Apple is the worst major US employer for turnover, even worse than Tesla. They also didn't plan for the amount of employees they have hired, so at most offices, including Apple Park HQ, there isn't enough parking, desk spac...
too many meetings, not a lot of work-life-balance Great team, poor work-life-balance

Some teams are downright misery, but transferring is usually a good option. Apple is huge and old, you will have to invest serious time to acquire institutional knowledge Good benefits, no work life balance
Long hours no work life balance rude customers Great company
Does not offer remote. They do not compromise on location. Good company to work for

In my org for the APP Store, there are no opportunities for growth or advancement. The most celebrated contribution is consistent high-volume production over all else. It's a dead-end job that goes no further than the operational role. Lack of advancement or growth
very bad for career growth, you feel very stagnant Interesting but slow
Some managers don't care or support career growth which is a huge part of the college program. Great place for development
Not a lot of room for career growth ( in terms of roles) however you will build skills Great place to work

evidence.role: Current employee - Software Development Engineer In Test (SDET)
evidence.text: - Stagnant growth - Low Base pkg Reliable and Diverse

Nepotism and favoritism thrive in the culture here. Example - manager hires mostly friends and then family/friends of those friends. Anything other than toxic positivity in the work place is targeted as bad teamwork. Benign hallway conversations about current events are weaponized. All new roles in Cupertino are being filled in San Diego or Austin with no room for growth in SCV. Regularly work over 40 hours/week. Cl...

Favoritism from management. They only hire part timers. Impossible to achieve "quotas", we don't have quotas but we have a point system for every product (including AppleCare+, Music, Fitness and other subscriptions). Beware of Work Politics

- Management is toxic and lacks accountability - Watching the company make billions year after year without proper pay for their employees regardless of tier does put a bad taste in your mouth - ability to move up within the company is dependent on likability and playing into store politics - CEO cozying up to THAT man also hurts the brand So so

Culture can easily shift to a toxic or fear-based culture. Almost everyone I saw who brought up hr violations was either stalled in career progress or fired. Too much of a focus on metrics while losing sight of the personal aspect of customer relations. Worth the experience but know when to get out

IS&T suffers from a lack of fairness and inclusivity. Indian managers often hire and convert only Indian contractors, and even full-time interviews seem biased. Overall, the system is being abused without oversight or accountability Hiring Abuse by Indian Managers
* Lack of support * Lot of work pressure * Management is not supportive Compiler SW engineer
Hard to get full time hours, lots of good employee never get full time. Good people
- No upward movement available - Weekends Required - Lower pay for new employees than current New Community Specialists are not being paid as well
